rulings.Case Title: RUTH CASH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and it¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action (labeled indemnity) and denied as to the second cause of action (labeled contribution). As to the first cause of action, defendant has satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs have not presented any evidence creating a triable fact that GM and Ford are entitled to complete indemnity from defendant since the undiputed fact is that any liability that defendant may have for Charles Cash¿s death is not the result of GM or Ford OEM parts attributable to defendant. As to the second cause of action, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the portion of the GM and Ford settlement payments attributable to economic damages was more than GM and Ford¿s proportional share of the total economic damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. rulings.Case Title: BILLY S. JOHNSON VS. ADVOCATE MINES, LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Paccar Inc
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff¿s motion to tax costs sought by defendant Paccar Inc. is continued to a date to be determined in Department 611 per the court¿s July 1 notification to the moving party that this motion needs to be heard by Judge McBride. The moving party must contact Department 611 to schedule a new hearing date and must inform Paccar Inc. of the new hearing date. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Defendant Aurora Pump Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant CSK Auto, Inc.¿s joinder to defendant Aurora Pumps Co.¿s motion to stay or dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. The underlying motion that CSK Auto sought to join was untimely. Aurora Pumps failed to provide two additional days notice for electronic service required by General Order No. 158, C.C.P. § 1010.6 and CRC 2.260. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CECIL RAY BRAWLEY VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s demurrer to the fourteenth (brake shoe grinding machine ¿ negligence) and fifteenth (brake shoe grinding machine ¿ strict liability, incorrectly numbered as the seventeenth by defendant) causes of action of plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained without leave to amend. The complaint fails to allege facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action against defendant, and amending the complaint cannot cure this deficiency. = (220/HEK)
